highlights:
# The Ciompi Uprising When Florence’s lowest-paid textile workers seized power, and how betrayal and ambition brought their revolution to a  ...    by -- Deirdre Pirro

Share  ...    Unrest had been fomenting in Florence, especially in the Santa Croce and Oltrarno neighbourhoods, before it broke out into what started, at least in the beginning, as a bloodless coup on June 24, 1378, which was led by the humblest of workers in the cloth industry, including day labourers, wool combers, dyers and those doing poorly paid jobs like the many shop boys employed in the trade. Known as the Ciompi, deriving from the French word compère (meaning “buddy” or  ...    The revolt was sparked by the election of Salvestro de’ Medici, a cousin of the banking branch of the family, who was nominated gonfalonier by popular acclaim on June 18, 1378.
